Interesting! The blocks changed significantly. Let me compare the initial state with the current state:
Initial state (from first analysis):
Row 17: [S, b, p, Y, p]
Row 20: [p, b]
Row 23: [g, S]
Row 26: [w, b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g]

Row 41: [g, b]

After 1 click on top red marker (17, 20):
Row 17: [b, p, Y, p, b]
Row 20: [S, S]
Row 23: [p, g]
Row 26: [w, b, g, w, S, p, w, S, g, g]
Row 29: [b, g]
Row 32: [w, p]
Row 35: [w, S, w, w, b, S, w, b, g, S]
Row 38: [b, b]
Row 41: [Y, g]
Row 44: [g, S, S, p, p]
Let me analyze the changes:
Row 17: [S, b, p, Y, p] → [b, p, Y, p, b] - looks like a left rotation, but the last element is b (copied from position 1) instead of S (the original first element)
Row 44: [S, S, p, p, g] → [g, S, S, p, p] - this IS a right rotation!
Row 20: [p, b] → [S, S] - completely different colors
Row 23: [g, S] → [p, g] - different
Row 29: [w, w] → [b, g] - different
Row 32: [w, g] → [w, p] - different
Row 38: [Y, w] → [b, b] - different
Row 41: [g, b] → [Y, g] - different
Row 26: [w, b, b, w, S, p, g, S, g, g] → [w, b, g, w, S, p, w, S, g, g] - positions 2, 6 changed (b→g, g→w)
Row 35: [w, S, b, w, b, S, p, b, g, S] → [w, S, w, w, b, S, w, b, g, S] - positions 2, 6 changed (b→w, p→w)
Hmm, the 2-block rows seem to be getting their colors from somewhere. Let me think about this differently.
Maybe the rows are organized into columns, and clicking the red marker shifts the colors within each column. The 2-block rows have blocks at cols 23-24 and 35-36. Let me call these column groups A and B.
Looking at column A (col 23-24) across all rows:
Initial: S, p, g, w, w, w, w, Y, g, S
After:  b, S, p, w, b, w, w, b, Y, g
Looking at column B (col 35-36) across all rows:
Initial: p, b, S, g, w, g, g, w, b, g
After:  b, S, g, g, g, p, S, b, g, p
Hmm, these don't look like simple rotations either.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the blocks are organized by their column positions, and clicking red shifts all blocks in each column group. Let me look at all column positions:
- Col 17-18: only in rows 26, 35
- Col 20-21: only in rows 26, 35
- Col 23-24: in rows 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44
- Col 26-27: in rows 17, 26, 35, 44
- Col 29-30: in rows 17, 26, 35, 44
- Col 32-33: in rows 17, 26, 35, 44
- Col 35-36: in rows 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44
- Col 38-39: in rows 26, 35
- Col 41-42: in rows 26, 35
- Col 44-45: in rows 26, 35
So there are 10 column groups. The marker rows (17, 26, 35) have blocks in more column groups than the non-marker rows.
Let me focus on col 23-24 (which appears in all rows):
Initial: S(17), p(20), g(23), w(26), w(29), w(32), w(35), Y(38), g(41), S(44)
After:   b(17), S(20), p(23), w(26), b(29), w(32), w(35), b(38), Y(41), g(44)
If I look at this as a vertical column of 10 elements:
Initial: [S, p, g, w, w, w, w, Y, g, S]
After:   [b, S, p, w, b, w, w, b, Y, g]
Hmm, this doesn't look like a simple rotation. Let me check col 35-36:
Initial: p(17), b(20), S(23), g(26), w(29), g(32), g(35), w(38), b(41), g(44)
After:   b(17), S(20), g(23), g(26), g(29), p(32), S(35), b(38), g(41), p(44)
Again, not a simple rotation.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the blocks are organized into "tracks" or "lanes" that wrap around. When I click the red marker, blocks move along these tracks.
Actually, let me look at the yellow blocks. They're at specific positions and might be "read/write" heads. Let me check their positions.
From the segmentation earlier:
Row ~25: Y, c, Y (3 objects, 1 pixel each for Y)
Row ~28: Y, c, Y
Row ~34: Y, c, Y
Row ~37: Y, c, Y
These are between the marker rows. They might indicate which columns are "active" for copying.
Let me try a different approach: just keep clicking the red markers and see if the level completes. In Level 1, 5 clicks completed the level. Maybe here too, a certain number of clicks on each red marker will complete the level.
But wait, there are 3 red markers. Maybe I need to click each one a certain number of times. Let me try clicking the top red marker a few more times and see the pattern.
Actually, let me first try clicking the green/N marker to see if it does the opposite (reverse rotation).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking the green marker for row 17 to see if it reverses
# Green marker center is at (17, 39)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 39}])
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

# Get current state
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

def get_row_blocks(row):
    line = ascii_lines[row]
    blocks = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(line):
        ch = line[i]
        if ch not in ('c', 'G', 'N', 'B', 'R'):
            j = i
            while j < len(line) and line[j] == ch:
                j += 1
            blocks.append((i, ch, j-i))
            i = j
        else:
            i += 1
    return blocks

for row in [17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44]:
    blocks = get_row_blocks(row)
    colors = [b[1] for b in blocks]
    print(f"Row {row}: {colors}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
